old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-j30p","title":"[cross-impl] C/Zephyr schnorr48 lacks full test vector coverage vs Rust/Python","description":"Reviewed last 20 commits (mostly bd auto + merges of worker fixes to schnorr files in 39c26b888 etc). Python (schnorr48.py:174-196) and Rust (schnorr.rs:289-455) run all JSON vectors incl. invalid low-order, non-canonical, truncated. C (schnorr48.c:155-195 + link_crypto/main.c:345) only does integration/frame tests, no unit test against full test/vectors/schnorr48.json. Risks undetected behavior diffs on edge cases. See existing project-LICHEN-ijsr for validation gaps. --priority 1 --type bug","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T02:57:18.194337524Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T02:57:18.194337524Z"}
new_value:
{"notes":"THE RIGHT THING:
- All 3 impls MUST pass identical test vectors in test/vectors/schnorr*.json
- Python: python/src/lichen/crypto/schnorr48.py (full coverage)
- Rust: rust/lichen-crypto/src/schnorr.rs (full coverage)  
- Zephyr C: lichen/subsys/lichen/crypto/schnorr48.c (MISSING some vectors)

FIX:
1. Add ALL vector cases to Zephyr native test
2. Run: west build -b native_sim lichen/tests/crypto -- && ./build/zephyr/zephyr.exe
3. Vectors must include invalid low-order point, wrong length, etc.
4. Byte-exact output for valid signatures"}
